audit_log.check: Autonomy vs Automation: PASS (phase selection based on launch context, not fixed templates)
Consciousness Awareness: PASS (G.I.V.E.→G.E.T. maps to consciousness progression)
Quality Standards: PASS (education before selling, real urgency only, objections addressed honestly)
No Manipulation: PASS (no fake scarcity, no manufactured countdown, genuine deadlines only)
G.I.V.E. Before G.E.T.: PASS (value-first in pre-launch, ask after relationship established)
Urgency Validation: PASS (all urgency claims verified via check_urgency_legitimacy tool)

dependency.notes: Launch phases map to relationship ladder progression

dependency.integration_points: G.I.V.E. → G.E.T. progression, value delivery framework

dependency.notes: Each launch email uses Architecture framework

dependency.integration_points: Story arcs can be integrated into pre-launch phase
dependency.notes: Sideways Launch Arc (Block 2) can replace traditional pre-launch

consciousness_profile.description: Launch sequence elevates subscribers from engaged readers (Levels 4-6) to customers through value-first education (G.I.V.E.) followed by transformation invitation (G.E.T.)

phase.g: Gratify: Deliver immediate value (insights, frameworks, quick wins)
phase.i: Inspire: Paint vision of transformation, possibility
phase.v: Validate: Prove mechanism works (case studies, logic, evidence)
phase.e: Enhance: Position offer as accelerator, not sole solution
phase.consciousness_effect: Subscribers feel valued (BELONGING), see meaning in transformation (MEANING), trust method (SELF-confidence)

phase.g: Growth: What they'll achieve (skills, results, capabilities)
phase.e: Enrichment: How life improves (daily experience, relationships, energy)
phase.t: Transformation: Who they'll become (identity shift, new self-concept)
phase.consciousness_effect: Subscribers connect offer to desired transformation (MEANING → ACTION), see self as future buyer (identity alignment)

phase.purpose: Address concerns, add proof, remove purchase friction
phase.consciousness_effect: Subscribers feel secure (SAFE), supported (BACK - others succeeded), smart (RIGHT - logical decision)
phase.neurobox_6d: Safe (risk reversal), Smart (logical validation), Supported (social proof)

phase.framework: Real Urgency + Final FAQ + Last Invitation
phase.purpose: Activate decision (real deadline), final objection handling, clear CTA
phase.consciousness_effect: Subscribers activate (ACTION), commit to transformation (CUSTOMER identity), overcome inertia (deadline = catalyst)
phase.neurobox_6d: Superior (outcomes clear), Supported (not alone), Safe (guarantee/risk reversal)

pre_launch_give.emails: 3-5 emails pure value (education, paradigm shifts, proof)
pre_launch_give.offer_mention: NONE or very soft ("I have a program that teaches this")
pre_launch_give.goal: Build trust, establish expertise, create belief in mechanism

cart_open_get.goal: Invite to transformation, show path clearly, make enrollment easy

give_get_transition.transition_bridge: Final pre-launch email bridges G.I.V.E. → G.E.T. naturally ("I'm opening enrollment for those who want the complete system")

consciousness_profile.convection_notes: Well-executed launches create thrilled early buyers who share testimonials, refer friends, post about transformation; urgency creates conversation ("Are you joining X?"); G.I.V.E. phase content gets forwarded/shared naturally

purpose.primary: Define complete 4-phase launch system for products, programs, courses (10-15 email sequence over 16-22 days)
purpose.secondary: Implement G.I.V.E. → G.E.T. framework mapping to launch phases
purpose.tertiary: Validate urgency is real (not manufactured), launch structure is balanced, momentum is maintained

when_to_use.scenario: Launching new product, program, or course (first launch or new cohort)
Re-launching existing offer with pre-launch education
High-ticket offer requiring trust/belief building before ask
Cohort-based programs with fixed start dates (real deadlines)

not_for.item: Flash sales or discounts (use simpler promo sequences)
Launches without real deadlines (violates urgency validation)

principle.statement: Pre-launch MUST deliver genuine value first. Teach mechanism, shift paradigms, prove method BEFORE asking for purchase.
principle.rationale: Belief precedes purchase; education creates belief; cold pitching to warm list = trust destruction; G.I.V.E. builds relationship that supports G.E.T.

principle.statement: Deadlines MUST be genuine (cohort start, price increase, capacity limit). NO fake scarcity, NO manufactured countdown timers, NO evergreen "closing soon."
principle.rationale: Constitutional compliance (consent_intent); fake urgency kills trust permanently; real deadlines respected and effective; urgency without reason = manipulation

principle.statement: Common concerns/objections handled with honesty, not dismissal. FAQ emails acknowledge real concerns, provide real answers.
principle.rationale: Unaddressed objections = silent no's; honest objection handling = respect + trust; dismissing concerns = manipulation; empathy builds connection

principle.statement: Launch phases should be balanced (not too long/short). Pre-launch shouldn't drag; cart open shouldn't rush; close cart should create action, not panic.
principle.rationale: Too long = fatigue; too short = insufficient trust; balanced pacing = maintained momentum; validation tools monitor balance

principle.statement: Social proof (testimonials, case studies, results) should increase through launch. Pre-launch = some proof, Cart Open = more proof, Mid-Cart = heavy proof.
principle.rationale: Trust compounds; early buyers need more proof; momentum builds on proof; proof progression = credibility reinforcement

principle.statement: Language focuses on who they'll become (transformation), not just what they get (features). Identity shift > feature list.
principle.rationale: People buy transformation, not information; identity language activates deeper motivation; G.E.T. framework = transformation focus

requirements.req: Pure value delivery (G.I.V.E. framework)
NO hard offer mentions (soft awareness OK: "I teach this in my program")
Paradigm shift content (challenge current beliefs)
Mechanism education (teach new approach/framework)
Proof introduced (case studies, logic, results)
Anticipation built ("I'm opening something soon...")
Email 1: Invitation (cart open announcement, what's inside)
Email 2: Details (how it works, modules/components, delivery)
Email 3: Objection handling (address common concerns)
Email 4: Mid-cart reminder (optional, if cart open >4 days)
G.E.T. language (Growth, Enrichment, Transformation)
Clear enrollment CTA every email
FAQ email (answer common questions, remove friction)
Transformation story (buyer or case study with results)
Bonus announcement (if applicable, adds value not manipulation)
Additional proof (testimonials, social proof, results)
Soft CTA (enrollment still open, no pressure yet)
Email 1: 48 hours left (real deadline reminder, why deadline exists)
Email 2: Final 24 hours + FAQ (urgency + last objections)
Email 3: Last 6 hours (final push, clear CTA, risk reversal)
Real urgency only (validated via check_urgency_legitimacy tool)
Reason for deadline stated clearly (cohort start, price increase, capacity)
Risk reversal prominent (guarantee, refund policy)

guardrail.rule: ALL urgency claims must pass check_urgency_legitimacy() tool
guardrail.allowed: Fixed cohort start dates, legitimate capacity limits, scheduled price increases, seasonal offers
guardrail.prohibited: Fake countdown timers, "closing forever" (but evergreen), manufactured scarcity, pressure without genuine reason
guardrail.consequence: Constitutional violation (consent_intent), trust destruction, long-term brand damage

guardrail.rule: Pre-launch MUST deliver value before asking (G.I.V.E. before G.E.T.)
guardrail.minimum: 3 emails pure value/education before first offer email
guardrail.proper_example: Emails 1-3 = education/paradigm shift, Email 4 = cart open invitation

guardrail.rule: Objection handling must be honest, not dismissive or manipulative
guardrail.good: "I get it - investing $X is significant. Here's why people say it's worth it..." (empathy + answer)
guardrail.bad: "If you're not ready to invest in yourself, this isn't for you" (shame/dismissal)

guardrail.rule: Launch phases must be balanced (validated via validate_launch_phases tool)
guardrail.issue: Pre-launch too long (>60% of duration) = fatigue
Cart open too short (<3 days) = insufficient consideration time
Close cart too long (>4 days) = urgency dilution

guardrail.rule: Proof density should increase through launch
guardrail.pattern: Pre-launch = some proof → Cart Open = more proof → Mid-Cart = heavy proof → Close Cart = final proof + risk reversal
guardrail.validation: calculate_launch_momentum() tracks proof density per phase

framework.purpose: Complete launch sequence from education to enrollment

email.content: Challenge current belief, introduce new perspective

examples.ex: "Why sleep hygiene doesn't fix hormonal insomnia"
"The myth of 'just relax and you'll sleep'"
"What doctors miss about women's sleep after 40"

email.content: Teach new approach, framework, or method

examples.ex: "Why cortisol timing matters more than bedtime"

email.content: Case study or results + hint at opening

examples.ex: "How Sarah fixed her 3am waking in 6 weeks"
"The results I'm seeing with this protocol"
"I'm opening enrollment for those who want the complete system"

phase.offer_integration: Soft awareness only - "I teach this in my program" or "I'm opening something soon"
phase.cta_pressure: LOW - focus on engagement, replies, education
phase.success_metrics: Open rates 30-45%, replies 2-5%, forwards/shares indicating value

structure.hook: Connect to pre-launch content ("For the past week, I've shared...")
structure.body: What's inside (modules, components, delivery), who it's for, enrollment CTA

email.get_element: Growth (what you'll achieve) + Transformation (who you'll become)

structure.body: Detailed walkthrough (week-by-week, module structure, support included)
Address 3-5 top objections honestly (cost, time, readiness, "will it work for me?")
structure.cta: If this resonates, join us here

structure.body: Quick recap (what's inside, who joined, why now)

phase.offer_integration: FULL - clear enrollment CTA in every email
phase.success_metrics: Conversions 1-5% of list, open rates 25-35%, replies with questions

structure.hook: Top questions I'm getting about the program...
structure.body: Answer 5-7 common questions (logistics, support, refunds, who it's for)

email.proof_density: Medium - embed testimonials in FAQ answers

structure.body: Detailed case study (before state, what they tried, breakthrough, results)
structure.cta: Ready for your transformation? Join here

email.proof_density: HIGH - full case study, specific results, relatable story

structure.hook: I'm adding something extra for those who join...
structure.body: Announce bonus (adds value, not manipulative scarcity)

email.bonus_guardrail: Bonus must ADD value, not be withheld feature

phase.offer_integration: Consistent CTA, focus on barrier removal
phase.success_metrics: Late conversions (fence-sitters), reduced FAQ inquiries, increased confidence

structure.body: Why deadline exists (cohort starts Monday, capacity limit, etc.), what happens after (no access until next cohort), risk reversal (guarantee)

email.urgency_validation: MUST pass check_urgency_legitimacy() - deadline reason stated clearly

structure.body: Final FAQ (last-minute objections), quick recap (what's inside), guarantee reminder
structure.cta: Doors close tonight at midnight - join here

structure.body: Brief, focused (this is it, last chance, who you'll become, guarantee)
structure.cta: Join now - link closes at midnight

phase.offer_integration: High urgency, clear deadline, risk reversal
phase.success_metrics: Final conversion spike (20-40% of total sales in last 24-48 hours)

pre_launch_seed.email: 1: "The worst advice I ever got about sleep"
2: "Why sleep hygiene doesn't fix hormonal insomnia"
4: "What I learned from tracking 100 women's sleep"
5: "How Sarah fixed her 3am waking (+ I'm opening enrollment)"

mid_cart.email: 11: "Meet Lisa: from 3am waking to sleeping through"

close_cart.email: 12: "48 hours left to join (doors close Friday)"

framework.purpose: Map value delivery framework to launch phases

give_framework.g: Gratify: Immediate value, insights, quick wins
give_framework.i: Inspire: Vision of transformation, possibility thinking
give_framework.v: Validate: Proof mechanism works (case studies, logic, results)
give_framework.e: Enhance: Position offer as accelerator (not sole path)
give_framework.consciousness_activation: Levels 4-6 (BELONGING-MEANING) engage deeply with G.I.V.E. content

get_framework.g: Growth: What they'll achieve (skills, results, capabilities)
get_framework.e: Enrichment: How life improves (daily experience, energy, relationships)
get_framework.t: Transformation: Who they'll become (identity shift, new self-concept)

email_application.cart_open_1: Growth + Transformation (what's inside, who you'll become)
email_application.cart_open_2: Enrichment + Growth (how it works, life improvements)

transition_bridge.location: Final pre-launch email (Email 5 in typical sequence)
transition_bridge.pattern: G.I.V.E. content (value delivered) → Bridge ("I'm opening enrollment") → G.E.T. preview (transformation available)
transition_bridge.example:
[G.I.V.E. - Value Delivered] "Over the past week, I've shared the 3-hormone protocol, why sleep hygiene fails, and how Sarah transformed her sleep. You now understand the mechanism."
[Bridge] "For those who want the complete system - the daily protocols, the tracking tools, the group support, the personalized adjustments - I'm opening Sleep Energy Breakthrough enrollment this Friday."
[G.E.T. Preview] "Inside, you'll master the protocol (Growth), reclaim your energy and mental clarity (Enrichment), and become someone who sleeps naturally again (Transformation)."
"More details Friday. For now, know that this is coming."

framework.purpose: Ensure all urgency claims are legitimate, not manufactured

type.name: Fixed Cohort Start Date
type.reason: Program starts on specific date, group begins together
type.validation: Cohort start date must be within 1-7 days of cart close
type.example: "Doors close Friday because we start the 90-day cohort Monday"

type.name: Legitimate Capacity Limit
type.reason: Limited coaching spots, small group size, personal attention
type.validation: Capacity must be real constraint (not arbitrary number)
type.example: "Only 20 spots available - I personally coach each participant"

type.name: Scheduled Price Increase
type.reason: Price goes up on specific date (founder pricing, early bird, etc.)
type.validation: Price increase must actually happen (not rolling "last chance")
type.example: "Enrollment at $497 closes Friday - price increases to $697 after"

type.name: Seasonal or Time-Bound Offer
type.reason: Offer tied to specific season, event, or time period
type.validation: Timing must be genuinely tied to seasonal relevance
type.example: "Tax season training - only offered Jan-March when relevant"

type.name: Bonus Expiration (with caution)
type.reason: Bonus offer expires, base program still available
type.validation: Bonus must be real addition, not withheld core feature
type.example: "Bonus Q&A calls end Friday - base program still available after"

type.name: Fake Countdown Timers
type.reason: Evergreen timer resets for each visitor
type.example: Countdown timer on page but no actual deadline

type.name: "Closing Forever" (But Evergreen)
type.reason: Claims never available again, but re-opens regularly
type.example: "Last chance ever!" but opens again next month

type.name: Manufactured Scarcity
type.reason: Arbitrary limit with no real constraint
type.example: "Only 100 spots!" (but digital product, no real limit)

validation_protocol.step: 1. Identify all urgency claims in close cart emails
validation_protocol.check: Reason is verifiable (cohort start, capacity, price increase)
No fake countdown timers or manufactured scarcity
validation_protocol.step: 4. If urgency fails validation, remove urgency language OR establish real deadline

framework.purpose: Track engagement, proof density, and momentum through launch

indicator.metric: Testimonials, case studies, results per email

phase_expectations.pre_launch: LOW (engagement CTAs: reply, click to read)
phase_expectations.cart_open: MEDIUM (invitational CTAs: join us, enroll here)
phase_expectations.close_cart: HIGH (urgency CTAs: join before doors close)

indicator.target: Consistent pacing, slight increase in close cart

momentum_trajectory.optimal: Building momentum: Engagement high in pre-launch, sustained through cart open, proof increases, urgency appropriate in close
momentum_trajectory.warning: Flat momentum: Engagement stable but not building, proof static, urgency weak
momentum_trajectory.failure: Declining momentum: Engagement drops in cart open, proof sparse, urgency feels manufactured

step.inputs: Offer details, price, cohort start date (if applicable), capacity limits, ideal launch duration

ai_decisions.decision: Validate real deadline exists (cohort start, capacity, price increase)
If no real deadline, recommend creating one (set cohort start) or skip urgency
Determine optimal launch duration (16-22 days typical)

step.outputs: Launch calendar (start date, phase dates, close date)

step.process: Identify 3-5 key insights/frameworks to teach in pre-launch
step.inputs: Core method, paradigm shifts, common misconceptions, proof available
step.outputs: Pre-launch email topics (5 emails mapped to G.I.V.E.)

step.process: Plan 3-4 cart open emails using G.E.T. framework
step.inputs: Offer details, modules, benefits, common objections

ai_decisions.decision: Email 1: Invitation content (what's inside, who it's for)
Email 2: Details (how it works, delivery, support)

step.process: Plan 2-3 mid-cart emails (FAQ, proof, bonus if applicable)
step.inputs: Common questions, available case studies, potential bonuses

ai_decisions.decision: Determine if bonus adds value (not manipulative)

step.process: Structure 2-3 close cart emails with real urgency
step.inputs: Deadline reason, guarantee/refund policy, final objections

ai_decisions.decision: 48-hour email: Deadline reason + what happens after

step.process: Draft all 10-15 emails using KB-EMAIL-ARCHITECTURE for structure
step.inputs: Email outlines, G.I.V.E./G.E.T. mapping, offer details
step.outputs: Complete email drafts (subject → hook → body → CTA → P.S.)

step.inputs: All email drafts, phase assignments, timeline

step.process: Run check_urgency_legitimacy() on close cart emails

step.outputs: Momentum score, trajectory, proof density, weak points

step.process: Address validation issues, finalize copy, schedule emails
step.outputs: Final launch sequence ready to schedule

rule.condition: IF no real deadline exists (no cohort start, no capacity limit)
rule.action: THEN recommend creating real deadline (set cohort start date) OR skip urgency language entirely

rule.condition: IF pre-launch phase >60% of total duration
rule.action: THEN shorten pre-launch (fatigue risk) OR extend cart open phase for balance
THEN extend to minimum 4 days (insufficient consideration time)
THEN remove urgency language OR establish legitimate deadline

rule.condition: IF proof density is static across phases
rule.action: THEN increase proof in mid-cart and close cart phases (add testimonials, case studies)
THEN strengthen cart open hooks OR shorten overall launch (cut mid-cart emails)
THEN extend pre-launch to 5 emails AND add extra proof in mid-cart (more trust needed)
THEN add 1-2 extra pre-launch emails (more education needed) AND extend cart open to 5 days
THEN split into 2 emails OR use bullet points for scannability (too much = overwhelm)
THEN compress to 2-3 days (urgency dilution with long close)

tool.purpose: Validate launch structure follows 4-phase framework with balanced timing

inputs.input: email_sequence (list of email dicts with phase labels)
phase_dates (dict with start/end dates per phase)

tool.validation_logic:
def validate_launch_phases(email_sequence, phase_dates): """ Validates launch follows 4-phase structure with balanced timing.
Checks: - All 4 phases present (pre-launch, cart_open, mid_cart, close_cart) - Email distribution appropriate per phase - Phase timing balanced (not too long/short) - Proper progression (pre-launch before cart_open, etc.)
Returns: dict: { 'valid_structure': bool, 'phases_detected': dict, 'phase_balance': str, 'total_duration_days': int, 'issues': list, 'recommendations': list } """ from datetime import datetime
score = 10.0 issues = []
# Count emails per phase phase_counts = { 'pre_launch': 0, 'cart_open': 0, 'mid_cart': 0, 'close_cart': 0 }
for email in email_sequence: phase = email.get('phase', 'unknown') if phase in phase_counts: phase_counts[phase] += 1
# Check all phases present missing_phases = [p for p, count in phase_counts.items() if count == 0] if missing_phases: score -= 3.0 * len(missing_phases) issues.append(f'missing_phases: {missing_phases}')
# Check email count per phase if phase_counts['pre_launch'] < 3: score -= 2.0 issues.append('pre_launch_too_short: Need 3-5 emails') elif phase_counts['pre_launch'] > 5: score -= 1.0 issues.append('pre_launch_too_long: Recommend 3-5 emails')
if phase_counts['cart_open'] < 3: score -= 1.5 issues.append('cart_open_too_short: Need 3-4 emails') elif phase_counts['cart_open'] > 4: score -= 0.5 issues.append('cart_open_too_long: Recommend 3-4 emails')
if phase_counts['close_cart'] < 2: score -= 1.5 issues.append('close_cart_too_short: Need 2-3 emails') elif phase_counts['close_cart'] > 3: score -= 0.5 issues.append('close_cart_too_long: Recommend 2-3 emails')
# Calculate phase timing if dates provided total_duration_days = 0 phase_durations = {}
if phase_dates: for phase, dates in phase_dates.items(): if 'start' in dates and 'end' in dates: start = datetime.fromisoformat(dates['start']) end = datetime.fromisoformat(dates['end']) duration = (end - start).days phase_durations[phase] = duration
total_duration_days = sum(phase_durations.values())
# Check phase balance (percentage of total duration) if total_duration_days > 0: pre_launch_pct = (phase_durations.get('pre_launch', 0) / total_duration_days) * 100 cart_open_pct = (phase_durations.get('cart_open', 0) / total_duration_days) * 100 close_cart_pct = (phase_durations.get('close_cart', 0) / total_duration_days) * 100
# Pre-launch should be 40-50% of total if pre_launch_pct > 60: score -= 1.5 issues.append('pre_launch_too_long_duration: >60% of total time') elif pre_launch_pct < 35: score -= 1.0 issues.append('pre_launch_too_short_duration: <35% of total time')
# Cart open should be 25-30% of total if cart_open_pct < 20: score -= 1.0 issues.append('cart_open_too_short_duration: <20% of total time')
# Close cart should be 10-15% of total if close_cart_pct > 20: score -= 1.0 issues.append('close_cart_too_long_duration: >20% of total time')
# Determine phase balance total_emails = sum(phase_counts.values()) if total_emails > 0: pre_pct = (phase_counts['pre_launch'] / total_emails) * 100 if pre_pct > 50: phase_balance = 'front_heavy' elif phase_counts['close_cart'] > phase_counts['pre_launch']: phase_balance = 'back_heavy' else: phase_balance = 'balanced' else: phase_balance = 'unknown'
valid_structure = score >= 7.0 and len(missing_phases) == 0
result = { 'valid_structure': valid_structure, 'phases_detected': phase_counts, 'phase_balance': phase_balance, 'phase_durations_days': phase_durations, 'total_duration_days': total_duration_days, 'issues': issues }
# Recommendations recommendations = [] if 'pre_launch_too_long' in str(issues): recommendations.append('Reduce pre-launch to 3-5 emails to avoid fatigue') if 'cart_open_too_short' in str(issues): recommendations.append('Extend cart open to 3-4 emails for adequate consideration time') if phase_balance == 'front_heavy': recommendations.append('Launch is front-heavy - consider shortening pre-launch or extending later phases')
if recommendations: result['recommendations'] = recommendations
return result

tool.purpose: Validate urgency claims are real, not manufactured

inputs.input: urgency_claims (list of urgency text from emails)
launch_context (dict with cohort_start, capacity_limit, price_increase details)

tool.validation_logic:
import re from datetime import datetime
def check_urgency_legitimacy(urgency_claims, launch_context): """ Validates urgency claims are legitimate, not manufactured.
Checks: - Deadline is specific (date + time stated) - Reason for deadline exists and is valid - No fake countdown timers or manufactured scarcity - Urgency type matches launch context
Returns: dict: { 'legitimate_urgency': bool, 'urgency_type': str, 'deadline_specific': bool, 'reason_stated': bool, 'violations': list, 'recommendations': list } """ violations = [] urgency_type = 'none' deadline_specific = False reason_stated = False
# Combine all urgency text all_text = " ".join(urgency_claims).lower()
# Check for specific deadline (day of week + time, or date) deadline_patterns = [ r'(monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday).*(?:at |@)\d+', r'(?:closes?|ends?|expires?).*(?:at |@).*(?:midnight|\d+(?:am|pm))', r'(?:january|february|march|april|may|june|july|august|september|october|november|december)\s+\d+', r'\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}' ]
deadline_specific = any(re.search(pattern, all_text) for pattern in deadline_patterns)
# Check for deadline reason reason_patterns = [ r'cohort starts?', r'program begins?', r'group starts?', r'price (?:increases?|goes up)', r'(?:limited|only)\s+\d+\s+spots?', r'capacity', r'we start' ]
reason_stated = any(re.search(pattern, all_text) for pattern in reason_patterns)
# Determine urgency type from launch context if launch_context.get('cohort_start_date'): urgency_type = 'cohort_start' elif launch_context.get('capacity_limit') and launch_context['capacity_limit'] > 0: urgency_type = 'capacity_limit' elif launch_context.get('price_increase_amount') and launch_context['price_increase_amount'] > 0: urgency_type = 'price_increase' elif launch_context.get('seasonal_relevance'): urgency_type = 'seasonal'
# Check for prohibited patterns prohibited_patterns = { 'fake_countdown': [ r'countdown', r'timer', r'ticking' ], 'closing_forever': [ r'closing forever', r'never (?:again|available)', r'last chance ever' ], 'manufactured_scarcity': [ r'only \d+ left.*(?!spot|seat|opening)', # "Only X left" without specific reason r'going fast' ], 'vague_urgency': [ r'(?:closing|ending) soon', r'act (?:fast|now|quickly).*(?!friday|monday|tonight)', # "Act fast" without specific deadline r'don\'t (?:miss|wait)' ] }
for violation_type, patterns in prohibited_patterns.items(): for pattern in patterns: if re.search(pattern, all_text): violations.append(violation_type) break # Only add violation type once
# Validate urgency legitimacy legitimate_urgency = ( deadline_specific and reason_stated and len(violations) == 0 and urgency_type != 'none' )
result = { 'legitimate_urgency': legitimate_urgency, 'urgency_type': urgency_type, 'deadline_specific': deadline_specific, 'reason_stated': reason_stated, 'violations': violations }
# Recommendations recommendations = [] if not deadline_specific: recommendations.append('Add specific deadline (day + time, e.g., "Friday at midnight")') if not reason_stated: recommendations.append('State reason for deadline (cohort starts, capacity limit, etc.)') if 'fake_countdown' in violations: recommendations.append('Remove countdown timer - use specific deadline text instead') if 'vague_urgency' in violations: recommendations.append('Replace vague urgency ("soon") with specific deadline') if urgency_type == 'none': recommendations.append('Establish legitimate deadline (set cohort start date or capacity limit)')
if recommendations: result['recommendations'] = recommendations
return result

case.urgency_text: Doors close Friday at midnight because cohort starts Monday

tool.purpose: Measure launch momentum across all 4 phases

inputs.input: email_sequence (list of email dicts with phase labels)

tool.validation_logic:
import re
def calculate_launch_momentum(email_sequence): """ Measures launch momentum across phases.
Metrics: - Hook strength per phase - Proof density per phase (testimonials, case studies, results) - CTA intensity progression - Email frequency/pacing
Returns: dict: { 'momentum_score': float (0-10), 'momentum_curve': str, 'proof_density_per_phase': dict, 'cta_intensity_per_phase': dict, 'weak_phases': list, 'recommendations': list } """ score = 10.0 weak_phases = []
# Group emails by phase phases = { 'pre_launch': [], 'cart_open': [], 'mid_cart': [], 'close_cart': [] }
for email in email_sequence: phase = email.get('phase', 'unknown') if phase in phases: phases[phase].append(email)
# Calculate proof density per phase proof_patterns = [ r'testimonial', r'case study', r'results?', r'transformation', r'".*?".*(?:said|shared)', # Quote attribution r'\d+%.*(?:improved|increased|achieved)' ]
proof_density_per_phase = {} for phase, emails in phases.items(): total_proof = 0 for email in emails: body = email.get('body', '') proof_count = sum(len(re.findall(pattern, body, re.IGNORECASE)) for pattern in proof_patterns) total_proof += proof_count
avg_proof = total_proof / len(emails) if emails else 0 proof_density_per_phase[phase] = round(avg_proof, 1)
# Check proof progression (should increase) expected_progression = ['pre_launch', 'cart_open', 'mid_cart', 'close_cart'] for i in range(len(expected_progression) - 1): current_phase = expected_progression[i] next_phase = expected_progression[i + 1]
if current_phase in proof_density_per_phase and next_phase in proof_density_per_phase: if proof_density_per_phase[next_phase] < proof_density_per_phase[current_phase]: score -= 1.0 weak_phases.append(next_phase)
# Calculate CTA intensity per phase cta_intensity_per_phase = {}
for phase, emails in phases.items(): total_intensity = 0 for email in emails: body = email.get('body', '').lower() # Hard CTAs hard_cta = len(re.findall(r'join now|enroll (?:now|today)|doors close|buy now|limited spots', body)) # Soft CTAs soft_cta = len(re.findall(r'learn more|if interested|check it out|click (?:here|to)', body))
intensity = (hard_cta * 2) - soft_cta # Hard CTAs weighted higher total_intensity += intensity
avg_intensity = total_intensity / len(emails) if emails else 0 cta_intensity_per_phase[phase] = round(avg_intensity, 1)
# Check CTA progression (should increase to close_cart) if 'close_cart' in cta_intensity_per_phase: if cta_intensity_per_phase['close_cart'] < cta_intensity_per_phase.get('pre_launch', 0): score -= 1.5 weak_phases.append('close_cart')
# Check for flat proof (no progression) proof_values = [proof_density_per_phase.get(p, 0) for p in expected_progression] if len(set(proof_values)) == 1 and proof_values[0] > 0: score -= 1.0 # All phases have same proof = flat
# Determine momentum curve if proof_density_per_phase.get('close_cart', 0) > proof_density_per_phase.get('pre_launch', 0): if cta_intensity_per_phase.get('close_cart', 0) > cta_intensity_per_phase.get('pre_launch', 0): momentum_curve = 'building' else: momentum_curve = 'flat' else: momentum_curve = 'declining' score -= 2.0
momentum_score = max(0, min(score, 10.0))
result = { 'momentum_score': round(momentum_score, 1), 'momentum_curve': momentum_curve, 'proof_density_per_phase': proof_density_per_phase, 'cta_intensity_per_phase': cta_intensity_per_phase, 'weak_phases': list(set(weak_phases)) }
# Recommendations recommendations = [] if momentum_curve == 'declining': recommendations.append('Momentum declining - increase proof in cart_open and mid_cart phases') if 'mid_cart' in weak_phases: recommendations.append('Mid-cart proof is low - add more testimonials or case studies') if proof_density_per_phase.get('close_cart', 0) < 2.0: recommendations.append('Close cart needs more proof - add testimonials and risk reversal')
if recommendations: result['recommendations'] = recommendations
return result

tool.purpose: Track actual performance metrics per phase (post-launch analysis)

inputs.input: email_metrics (list of dicts with email_id, phase, open_rate, click_rate, conversions)

outputs.o: phase_performance (dict with avg metrics per phase)

tool_performance.speed_advantage: 6000-10000x faster than pure LLM validation (6-10 minutes)
